features.summary: Nike reported Q2 fiscal 2022 results with 1% revenue growth despite supply chain disruptions from Vietnam factory closures that eliminated 130 million units of production. Digital business grew 11% and now represents 25% of total Nike brand revenue, with over 79 million engaged members across the ecosystem. North America performed strongly with 12% revenue growth, while Greater China declined 24% due to supply constraints. Gross margin expanded 280 basis points driven by higher full-price realization and lower markdowns, though this was partially offset by increased freight costs.
features.management_outlook: Management maintains mid-single digit revenue growth guidance for fiscal 2022 and raised gross margin guidance to 150 basis points expansion. They expect low-single digit revenue growth in Q3 due to ongoing Vietnam production impacts but anticipate supply normalization heading into fiscal 2023. The company remains confident in its Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy and fiscal 2025 targets, with inventory supply expected to normalize and sequential improvement beginning in Q3, particularly in Greater China.
features.risks: Primary risks include ongoing supply chain disruptions from Vietnam factory closures, COVID-related volatility including potential Omicron variant impacts, and inventory supply significantly lagging consumer demand across all regions. Greater China faces additional challenges from local COVID lockdowns affecting 25% of partner retail stores and competitive pressures in the market. Rising input costs and supply chain expenses are expected to pressure margins in the second half of the fiscal year.
features.key_drivers: Digital transformation continues as the primary growth driver with 11% digital growth and 40% of total digital demand coming from mobile apps. Member engagement grew 27% with repeat buyers up 50%, driving higher average selling prices and order values. The company's direct-to-consumer strategy is gaining traction with Nike Direct reaching 48% of total North America marketplace. Strong brand momentum evidenced by record Black Friday performance and successful product launches, supported by investments in innovation and athlete partnerships.
